hero.description: An academically rigorous, AI-driven housing prediction model. We estimate and contextualize property values across real estate markets using verifiable metrics and transparent algorithms.

methodology.title: Under the Hood
methodology.description: Our approach prioritizes explainability. We avoid black-box techniques in favor of structured ensemble methods where each variable's weight can be audited and traced back to verifiable real-world phenomena.

dataIngestion.title: Data Ingestion
dataIngestion.desc: Aggregating historical property price data, demographic factors (age, immigration), and macro-economic indicators (IPC, mortgages) from the INE.

featureExtraction.title: Feature Extraction
featureExtraction.desc: Computing 12-month trailing lag features and macroeconomic variations to construct a robust temporal context for each region.

dnn.title: Deep Neural Network
dnn.desc: A PyTorch-based Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) trained on historical cycles to predict 12-month property value dynamics with high confidence.

estimator.title: Model Inference
estimator.subtitle: // Calculates projected valuation 12 months into the future using INE macro-economic data and a PyTorch Neural Network.
estimator.provide: Provide the spatial parameters to calculate the estimated local valuation.
estimator.error: Error connecting to neural engine / API.

analysts.title: For Analysts
analysts.desc: Analyze structural shifts in local real estate markets using verifiable INE data on inflation and mortgages to build comprehensive quarterly reports.

buyers.title: For Buyers
buyers.desc: Gain an academic projection on future house pricing across autonomous communities to inform better long-term residential investments.

risk.title: Risk Mitigation
risk.desc: Understand how macroeconomic forces (like changing mortgage rates and age demographics) will likely stress local housing demand over the next 12 months.

metric.title: Core System Metric
metric.desc: The model incorporates the latest macroeconomic updates from the INE, ensuring inference relies on current market reality rather than stale historical baselines.

q1.q: What data structures support the prediction?
q1.a: A tensor approach incorporating tabular economic indicators (IPC, mortgages), demographic data, and historical pricing dynamics.

q2.q: How does the platform handle missing variables?
q2.a: Missing INE data points or API fallbacks are gracefully handled using established regional baselines to ensure continuous inference.

footer.desc: The AI-Powered Real Estate Platform built for scale, reliability, and precision.
